channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Many Paths to Recovery: The Case for Treatment Pluralism.
item.description: This synopsis argues for treatment pluralism in musculoskeletal pain conditions, noting that decades of research show various interventions like exercise and manual techniques have modest, broadly equivalent effects likely driven by nonspecific and contextual mechanisms.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term risk of knee replacement after ACL reconstruction using the contralateral knee as an internal control: a National Hospital Episode Statistics database study of 135 881 patients
item.description: A large population-based study (N=135,881) using national hospital data found that patients who underwent ACL reconstruction had a 4.9 times higher long-term risk of knee arthroplasty compared to their contralateral knee.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Is Knee Osteoarthritis an Inevitable Consequence for Athletes after Anterior Cruciate Ligament Injury?
item.description: This article highlights that anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury should be viewed as a long-term knee joint health condition that may lead to post-traumatic knee osteoarthritis, despite advances in surgical reconstruction.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smartphone-Based Semi-Immersive Virtual Reality Enhances Upper Extremity Function in Subacute Stroke: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial (N=40) demonstrated that smartphone-based semi-immersive virtual reality augmented conventional physical therapy significantly enhanced upper extremity function in individuals with subacute stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: THE COMPARATIVE EFFECTIVENESS OF INPATIENT VERSUS SKILLED NURSING FACILITY REHABILITATION USING LINKED ACUTE STROKE REGISTRY DATA.
item.description: A retrospective comparative effectiveness cohort study examined rehabilitation outcomes in inpatient rehabilitation facilities versus skilled nursing facilities for acute stroke patients, using linked registry data to assess 'home time' as a proxy for functional recovery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Are There Sex Disparities in Return to Sport After Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction? A Systematic Review and Exploratory Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and exploratory meta-analysis investigated sex disparities in return-to-sport (RTS) outcomes after anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction (ACLR), noting that female athletes may have higher ACL injury rates but lower RTS rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of maintenance interventions promoting physical activity following exercise therapy for chronic conditions: an umbrella review
item.description: An umbrella review investigated the effectiveness of maintenance interventions following exercise therapy for chronic conditions on physical activity, function, fitness, and quality of life, using a narrative synthesis.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The impact of a brief online education intervention on the knowledge, attitudes and beliefs of qualified physiotherapists about chronic low back pain: Pre- and post-intervention survey.
item.description: A pre- and post-intervention survey (N=74) found that a brief online education program improved physiotherapists' knowledge, attitudes, and beliefs regarding chronic low back pain, supporting biopsychosocial approaches.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Developing consensus on competency-based educational standards in orthopaedic manual physical therapy fellowship training: part 2: knowledge for specialty practice and professionalism.
item.description: This study is part of a series aiming to establish competency-based educational standards for orthopaedic manual physical therapy fellowship training, focusing on the knowledge required for specialty practice and professionalism.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of passive vs. assistive robotic gait training on functional recovery and neuroplasticity post-stroke: A randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial (N=48) compared passive versus assistive robotic gait training in subacute stroke patients, investigating their impacts on lower limb motor function recovery and neural mechanisms.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Core Stability Training for Post-Stroke Recovery: A Multifaceted Approach to Enhance Postural Control and Motor Function.
item.description: This review explores Core Stability Training (CST) as a multifaceted approach to enhance postural control and motor function in stroke survivors, who commonly experience deficits in these areas.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association between Patellofemoral Conditions and the Incidence of ACL Injury: Could Patellofemoral Conditions be a Risk Factor for ACL Injury? A 10-year Population-Based Study.
item.description: A 10-year population-based study investigated the association between patellofemoral conditions and the incidence of ACL injury, exploring whether patellofemoral conditions could be a risk factor.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Functional stratification reveals speed-independent gait impairments beyond chronological age.
item.description: This study found that functionally stratifying older adults using the Short Physical Performance Battery revealed speed-independent gait impairments beyond chronological age, indicating early functional decline.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Influence of environmental barriers and facilitators on functioning: Analytical observational study in community-dwelling older adults.
item.description: A cross-sectional observational study analyzed the influence of environmental barriers and facilitators on the functioning of community-dwelling older adults using standardized, quantitative instruments.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effect of a multimodal exercise training program on sleep in older adults: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial (N=120) investigated the effect of a multimodal exercise training program on sleep in older adults, finding improvements in sleep onset latency, sleep efficiency, and daytime function.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association Between Postoperative Mini-Balance Evaluation Systems Test Scores and Length of Hospital Stay in Patients with Chronic Subdural Hematoma: A Prospective Cohort Study.
item.description: This prospective cohort study found an association between postoperative Mini-Balance Evaluation Systems Test (Mini-BESTest) scores and length of hospital stay in patients with chronic subdural hematoma.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Barriers and facilitators to leisure-time physical activity among individuals with spinal cord injury in China: A qualitative study.
item.description: This qualitative study explored barriers and facilitators to leisure-time physical activity among 20 community-dwelling adults with spinal cord injury in China, using the COM-B model.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Accelerometry-derived physical activity following Achilles tendon rupture: A prospective cohort study.
item.description: This prospective cohort study investigated accelerometry-derived physical activity during the first year after Achilles tendon rupture (n=42), exploring its relationship with patient-reported outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Lung Boost Trainer Versus Incentive Spirometer on Pulmonary Function Post COVID Hemiplegic Cerebral Palsy Children; Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: This RCT compared the efficacy of Lung Boost Trainer versus incentive spirometer on pulmonary function in COVID hemiplegic cerebral palsy children, finding the trainer to be superior.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative effectiveness of supervised individual, group-based, and mixed physiotherapy approaches on pain and function in patients with chronic pain in primary care: a retrospective observational study.
item.description: A retrospective observational study (N=220) compared individual, group-based, and mixed physiotherapy for chronic pain, finding similar improvements in pain and function across all three approaches in primary care.
item.author: PubMed
